primary.usage: Headings, Power Level, Level Titles, Hero Text

secondary.usage: Body text, labels, button text, descriptions

PrimaryButton.usage: Use expo-linear-gradient as background. Ensure text is dark (#0A0A0A) on gold.

DrumInput.usage: OTP screen 6-digit inputs. Render as individual styled cells.

ScreenOverlay.usage: CRITICAL: DO NOT use complete transparent background in header, overlays, or any surface that has content under it. Must use deep dark semi-transparent like rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.9).

motion.transitions: Use react-native-reanimated or Animated for smooth transitions. Avoid heavy Javascript-driven animations.
motion.interactions: Use TouchableOpacity with activeOpacity={0.7} for standard button presses. Buttons should feel responsive and tactical.

accessibility.touchTargets: All interactive elements MUST be at least 44x44px.
accessibility.contrast: Maintain high contrast. Gold on dark background is good, but text on gold MUST be #0A0A0A for readability.
accessibility.test_id: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a testID attribute (React Native equivalent of data-testid) to facilitate robust automated testing.

media.images: Use React Native <Image /> with appropriate resizeMode (cover for backgrounds, contain for icons). Background images should have a dark overlay to maintain text readability.

image_urls.description: Golden glitter sparkles with a dark background for the welcome/onboarding splash screen.
Dark, moody fitness image for the advanced body training tier.
Stoic statue background to be used behind daily wisdom quotes.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a React Native application. DO NOT use HTML elements (div, span, button, etc.).
DO NOT use Tailwind classes (className). Use React Native StyleSheet.
All screens MUST be wrapped in SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context.
Use KeyboardAvoidingView with behavior='padding' for any screen containing text inputs.
Colors are pre-defined in this guideline. Use the exact hex values or gradient stops.
For the gold gradient, use LinearGradient from 'expo-linear-gradient' with colors={['#8A6420', '#C8A96E', '#F0D090']} start={{x: 0, y: 0}} end={{x: 1, y: 0}}.
Never hardcode colors in components. Create a central ThemeContext or constants file for easy switching between dark/light mode.
Test IDs are required. Use `testID='your-kebab-case-id'` instead of `data-testid` since this is React Native.
For icons, exclusively use Feather from @expo/vector-icons as instructed.
Ensure the gold color is used for active states and accents, while the background stays heavily dark (#0A0A0A) to maintain the premium feel.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Avoid generic centered layouts. Use left alignment for readability in data-heavy cards.
Create depth through border colors and subtle shadows, rather than just solid grey backgrounds.
Never use AI assistant Emoji characters. Rely on Feather icons.
Use 8pt grid spacing system. Use padding/margins like 8, 16, 24, 32.
Buttons should have active feedback. Use TouchableOpacity with activeOpacity={0.8}.
Prioritize optical alignment over geometric alignment.
CRITICAL: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a testID attribute (e.g. testID='login-button') to facilitate robust automated testing.
